sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') AS source_site, json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') AS destination_site, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size_bytes, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_throughput_bps FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY source_site, destination_site ORDER BY avg_throughput_bps ASC

question: For FileRead Started events grouped into simulation time buckets of 10000 time units, what is the count of read operations initiated, average declared file size, and average disk read bandwidth per bucket — revealing when local disk read activity peaks and whether declared read performance degrades under high concurrent I/O pressure during the simulation?
sql: SELECT CAST(TIME / 10000 AS INTEGER) * 10000 AS time_bucket, COUNT(*) AS read_starts, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size_bytes, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_disk_read_bw_bps FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Started' GROUP BY time_bucket ORDER BY time_bucket

question: For FileTransfer Started events grouped by destination site, what is the transfer count, average and maximum site storage utilization, and average and maximum grid storage utilization at transfer initiation — revealing which sites most frequently receive inbound data transfers onto already heavily loaded local and grid-wide storage systems?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') AS destination_site, COUNT(*) AS inbound_transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_storage_util, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS max_site_storage_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_storage_util, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS max_grid_storage_util FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Started' GROUP BY destination_site ORDER BY max_site_storage_util DESC

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped into simulation time buckets of 10000 time units, what is the job count, total cost, average cost, and average duration per bucket — revealing how compute expenditure and job throughput evolve across the simulation lifecycle and whether cost concentrates in early or late simulation phases?
sql: SELECT CAST(TIME / 10000 AS INTEGER) * 10000 AS time_bucket, COUNT(*) AS job_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS total_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration_sec FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY time_bucket ORDER BY time_bucket

question: For completed FileTransfer events, what are the top 10 files by total bytes transferred, showing transfer count, total bytes transferred, average duration, and average link load — identifying the most data-heavy files moving across the grid and whether the most frequently transferred objects tend to travel over congested network links?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file') AS file, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_transferred, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY file ORDER BY total_bytes_transferred DESC LIMIT 10

question: For completed JobExecution events, what is the distribution of jobs by retry count bucket (0 retries, 1 retry, 2 retries, 3 or more retries) per site, along with the average execution duration per bucket — revealing which sites experience the highest re-execution rates and whether repeated retries correlate with longer runtimes?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) = 0 THEN '0_retries' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) = 1 THEN '1_retry' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) = 2 THEN '2_retries' ELSE '3_or_more_retries' END AS retry_bucket, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS FLOAT)) AS avg_duration_seconds FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site, retry_bucket ORDER BY site, retry_bucket

question: For completed FileTransfer events grouped by link_load buckets (0.00–0.25, 0.25–0.50, 0.50–0.75, 0.75–1.00), what is the transfer count, average file size in bytes, average duration, and average effective throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration) — revealing how rising network congestion systematically degrades transfer performance across the grid?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS FLOAT) < 0.25 THEN '0.00-0.25' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS FLOAT) < 0.50 THEN '0.25-0.50' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS FLOAT) < 0.75 THEN '0.50-0.75' ELSE '0.75-1.00' END AS link_load_bucket, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS FLOAT)) AS avg_file_size_bytes, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS FLOAT)) AS avg_duration_seconds, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS FLOAT) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS FLOAT)) AS avg_throughput_bytes_per_sec FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY link_load_bucket ORDER BY link_load_bucket

question: For completed FileRead events grouped by site and disk device, what is the read count, total bytes read, average read duration, maximum read duration, and average disk read bandwidth — identifying which individual storage devices are the most active and most latency-prone for local read operations across the entire grid?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk') AS disk, COUNT(*) AS read_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS INTEGER)) AS total_bytes_read, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS FLOAT)) AS avg_read_duration_seconds, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS FLOAT)) AS max_read_duration_seconds, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS FLOAT)) AS avg_disk_read_bw_bytes_per_sec FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site, disk ORDER BY total_bytes_read DESC

question: For completed FileTransfer events grouped by source site, what is the transfer count, average declared bandwidth, average effective throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), and the average bandwidth utilization ratio (effective throughput divided by declared bandwidth) — revealing which outbound network links are closest to saturation and which sites chronically underutilize their declared network capacity?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') AS source_site, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS FLOAT)) AS avg_declared_bandwidth_bytes_per_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS FLOAT) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS FLOAT)) AS avg_effective_throughput_bytes_per_sec, AVG((CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS FLOAT) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS FLOAT)) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS FLOAT), 0)) AS avg_bandwidth_utilization_ratio FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY source_site ORDER BY avg_bandwidth_utilization_ratio DESC

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped by site and host, what is the job count, average declared flops, average execution duration, average effective compute rate in flops per second (declared flops divided by duration), and average declared CPU speed — identifying which individual compute nodes deliver the highest and lowest effective processing rates relative to their own declared hardware speed?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, json_extract(METADATA, '$.host') AS host, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS FLOAT)) AS avg_declared_flops, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS FLOAT)) AS avg_duration_seconds, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS FLOAT) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS FLOAT), 0)) AS avg_effective_flops_per_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.speed') AS FLOAT)) AS avg_declared_speed_flops_per_sec FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site, host ORDER BY avg_effective_flops_per_sec DESC

question: For completed FileWrite events grouped by site and disk device, what is the write count, total bytes written, average write duration, maximum write duration, and average disk write bandwidth — identifying which physical storage devices across the grid handle the heaviest write workloads and which show the highest per-operation latency?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk') AS disk, COUNT(*) AS write_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_written, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_write_duration, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS max_write_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_disk_write_bw FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site, disk ORDER BY total_bytes_written DESC

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped by site, what is the average difference between site CPU utilization and grid CPU utilization, the maximum such difference, and the count and fraction of jobs where site CPU utilization exceeds grid CPU utilization by more than 0.2 — revealing which sites regularly function as local CPU hotspots even when the broader grid has spare capacity?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS total_jobs, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL) - CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_minus_grid_cpu_util, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL) - CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS max_site_minus_grid_cpu_util, SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL) - CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL) > 0.2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS hotspot_job_count, CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL) - CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL) > 0.2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / COUNT(*) AS hotspot_fraction FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_site_minus_grid_cpu_util DESC

question: For completed FileTransfer events grouped by declared bandwidth tier (below 100000000 bytes per second, 100000000 to 1000000000 bytes per second, and 1000000000 bytes per second and above), what is the transfer count, average file size in bytes, average duration, average effective throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), and average bandwidth utilization ratio (effective throughput divided by declared bandwidth) — revealing whether higher-bandwidth links deliver proportionally better transfer performance or whether congestion and latency offset their capacity advantage?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL) < 100000000 THEN 'below_100000000_bps' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL) < 1000000000 THEN '100000000_to_1000000000_bps' ELSE '1000000000_bps_and_above' END AS bandwidth_tier, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size_bytes, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_effective_throughput_bps, AVG((CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_bandwidth_utilization_ratio FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY bandwidth_tier

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped by declared core count bucket (1 core, 2 to 4 cores, 5 to 8 cores, more than 8 cores), what is the job count, average flops, average execution duration, and average effective compute rate in flops per second (flops divided by duration) — revealing how declared job parallelism relates to actual computational throughput and whether multi-core jobs achieve proportionally faster execution?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cores') AS INTEGER) = 1 THEN '1_core' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cores') AS INTEGER) <= 4 THEN '2_to_4_cores' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cores') AS INTEGER) <= 8 THEN '5_to_8_cores' ELSE 'more_than_8_cores' END AS cores_bucket, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cores') AS REAL)) AS avg_cores, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)) AS avg_flops, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_effective_compute_rate_flops_per_sec FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY cores_bucket ORDER BY MIN(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cores') AS INTEGER))

question: For completed FileRead events, what are the top 10 most frequently re-read files, showing read count, count of distinct sites where the file was read, average file size in bytes, average read duration, and average disk read bandwidth — identifying the hottest data objects in the simulation and whether widely shared files show degraded local read performance across the grid?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file') AS file, COUNT(*) AS read_count, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) AS distinct_site_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_size_bytes, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_read_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_disk_read_bw FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY file ORDER BY read_count DESC LIMIT 10

question: For completed JobExecution events per site, what is the average ratio of total_io_read_time to execution duration (I/O fraction of runtime) and the average ratio of file_transfer_queue_time to total_queue_time (file-staging fraction of queue time), alongside job count — revealing which sites are most I/O-bound versus compute-bound and where file-staging delays dominate scheduling overhead?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_io_read_time') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_io_fraction_of_runtime, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.file_transfer_queue_time') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_staging_fraction_of_queue FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_io_fraction_of_runtime DESC

question: For completed FileTransfer events grouped by source and destination site pair, what is the transfer count, minimum latency, average latency, maximum latency, and fraction of transfers where link_load exceeds 0.8 — revealing which inter-site network routes combine the highest latency with the most frequent near-saturation congestion?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') AS source_site, json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') AS destination_site, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, MIN(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.latency') AS REAL)) AS min_latency, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.latency') AS REAL)) AS avg_latency, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.latency') AS REAL)) AS max_latency, AVG(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL) > 0.8 THEN 1.0 ELSE 0.0 END) AS fraction_high_link_load FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY source_site, destination_site ORDER BY avg_latency DESC

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped by site and host, what is the job count, average resource_waiting_queue_time, maximum resource_waiting_queue_time, and average execution duration — identifying which individual compute nodes impose the longest resource-contention waits before execution begins and whether high wait times correlate with longer runtimes?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, json_extract(METADATA, '$.host') AS host, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.resource_waiting_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_resource_wait, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.resource_waiting_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS max_resource_wait, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_execution_duration FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site, host ORDER BY avg_resource_wait DESC

question: For completed JobExecution events where retries is greater than zero, grouped by site, what is the count of retried jobs, average retry count, maximum retry count, and average cost per retry (cost divided by retries) — revealing which sites experience the most persistent job failures and how much extra compute cost each retry cycle imposes per site?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS retried_job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS REAL)) AS avg_retries, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER)) AS max_retries, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_cost_per_retry FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' AND CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) > 0 GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_retries DESC

question: For completed FileWrite events grouped by site, what is the write count, total bytes written, average write duration, average site_storage_util, maximum site_storage_util, and the difference between average site_storage_util and average grid_storage_util — revealing which sites sustain the heaviest local storage pressure during writes and which consistently run hotter than the grid-wide storage average?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS write_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_written, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_write_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_storage_util, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS max_site_storage_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) - AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS site_vs_grid_storage_delta FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_site_storage_util DESC

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped by site, what is the job count, average ratio of resource_waiting_queue_time to total_queue_time, fraction of jobs where that ratio exceeds 0.5, and average grid CPU utilization at completion — revealing which sites are dominated by resource-contention waits versus file-staging delays and whether grid-wide CPU pressure is the underlying driver?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.resource_waiting_queue_time') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_resource_wait_fraction, SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.resource_waiting_queue_time') AS REAL) > 0.5 * CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 1.0 / COUNT(*) AS frac_resource_wait_dominant, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_resource_wait_fraction DESC

question: For completed FileRead events grouped into file size buckets (below 1000000 bytes, 1000000 to 100000000 bytes, 100000000 to 1000000000 bytes, and 1000000000 bytes and above), what is the read count, average read duration, and average effective read throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration) — revealing how local disk read performance scales with file size and whether large files sustain higher or lower throughput than small files?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) < 1000000 THEN 'under_1000000' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) < 100000000 THEN '1000000_to_100000000' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) < 1000000000 THEN '100000000_to_1000000000' ELSE '1000000000_and_above' END AS size_bucket, COUNT(*) AS read_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_effective_read_bps FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY size_bucket ORDER BY MIN(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL))

question: For each site, by matching completed JobAllocation events to the corresponding JobExecution Started event on the same JOB_ID, what is the job count, average, minimum, and maximum elapsed time between allocation completion and execution start — revealing which sites impose the longest pre-execution staging delays between resource assignment and the onset of actual computation?
sql: SELECT json_extract(je.METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(je.TIME - ja.TIME) AS avg_staging_gap, MIN(je.TIME - ja.TIME) AS min_staging_gap, MAX(je.TIME - ja.TIME) AS max_staging_gap FROM EVENTS ja JOIN EVENTS je ON ja.JOB_ID = je.JOB_ID WHERE ja.EVENT = 'JobAllocation' AND ja.STATE = 'Finished' AND je.EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND je.STATE = 'Started' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_staging_gap DESC

question: For completed FileTransfer events grouped by destination site, what is the transfer count, average file size in bytes, average transfer duration, average effective throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), and average link load — revealing which sites are the busiest data recipients and whether inbound transfers to high-volume destinations consistently suffer elevated network congestion?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') AS destination_site, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_effective_throughput_bps, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY destination_site ORDER BY transfer_count DESC

question: For completed FileWrite events grouped into grid_storage_util buckets (0.00–0.25, 0.25–0.50, 0.50–0.75, 0.75–1.00), what is the write count, average effective write throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), average declared disk_write_bw, and average write bandwidth utilization ratio (effective throughput divided by declared disk_write_bw) — revealing whether rising grid-wide storage pressure degrades actual disk write performance relative to declared device capacity?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL) < 0.25 THEN '0.00-0.25' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL) < 0.50 THEN '0.25-0.50' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL) < 0.75 THEN '0.50-0.75' ELSE '0.75-1.00' END AS grid_storage_util_bucket, COUNT(*) AS write_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_effective_write_bps, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_write_bw, AVG((CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_write_bw_utilization_ratio FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY grid_storage_util_bucket ORDER BY grid_storage_util_bucket

question: For completed FileRead events grouped by site and host, what is the read count, count of distinct files read, count of distinct disk devices used, total bytes read, average read duration, and average disk read bandwidth — identifying which individual compute nodes perform the most local disk I/O and whether hosts drawing from multiple disk devices achieve higher aggregate read throughput?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, json_extract(METADATA, '$.host') AS host, COUNT(*) AS read_count, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file')) AS distinct_files, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk')) AS distinct_disks, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_read, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_read_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_disk_read_bw FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site, host ORDER BY read_count DESC

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped by site, what is the job count, average job cost, average cost per declared flop (cost divided by flops), average cost per unit of execution time (cost divided by duration), and maximum single-job cost — revealing which sites deliver the best and worst computational cost efficiency and where the most expensive individual jobs are concentrated?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_cost_per_flop, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_cost_per_second, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS max_cost FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_cost_per_flop DESC

question: For completed FileTransfer events grouped into file size buckets (below 1000000 bytes, 1000000 to 100000000 bytes, 100000000 to 1000000000 bytes, and 1000000000 bytes and above), what is the transfer count, average declared bandwidth, average network latency, average transfer duration, and average effective throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration) — revealing how file size relates to observed network performance and whether larger files better exploit available link capacity?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) < 1000000 THEN 'A_below_1MB' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) < 100000000 THEN 'B_1MB_to_100MB' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) < 1000000000 THEN 'C_100MB_to_1GB' ELSE 'D_1GB_and_above' END AS size_bucket, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_bandwidth, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.latency') AS REAL)) AS avg_latency, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_effective_throughput_bps FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY size_bucket ORDER BY size_bucket

question: For FileWrite Started events grouped by site, what is the write count, average and maximum site_storage_util, average and maximum grid_storage_util, and the fraction of write operations initiated when site_storage_util exceeds 0.75 — revealing which sites most frequently begin write operations onto already heavily pressured local storage and whether local pressure tracks grid-wide storage saturation?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS write_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_storage_util, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS max_site_storage_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_storage_util, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS max_grid_storage_util, AVG(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL) > 0.75 THEN 1.0 ELSE 0.0 END) AS fraction_high_util_writes FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Started' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_site_storage_util DESC

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped by host, what is the job count, average and maximum total_queue_time, average fraction of total_queue_time attributable to file_transfer_queue_time, and average fraction attributable to resource_waiting_queue_time — identifying which individual compute nodes impose the longest scheduling delays and whether those delays are primarily driven by data-staging overhead or resource contention?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.host') AS host, json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_total_queue_time, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS max_total_queue_time, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.file_transfer_queue_time') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_file_transfer_fraction, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.resource_waiting_queue_time') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_resource_waiting_fraction FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY host, site ORDER BY avg_total_queue_time DESC

question: For completed JobExecution events, what are the top 10 individual jobs by cost, showing job ID, site, host, declared flops, execution duration, total cost, retry count, and grid CPU utilization at completion — identifying the single most expensive compute tasks in the simulation and whether high cost correlates with long duration, repeated retries, or elevated grid-wide CPU pressure?
sql: SELECT JOB_ID, json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, json_extract(METADATA, '$.host') AS host, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL) AS flops, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) AS duration, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL) AS cost, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) AS retries, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL) AS grid_cpu_util FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' ORDER BY CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL) DESC LIMIT 10

question: For completed FileTransfer events grouped by whether source_site equals destination_site (intra-site) or differs (inter-site), what is the transfer count, average file size in bytes, average duration, average effective throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), and average link load — revealing whether intra-site data movement achieves meaningfully higher throughput and lower congestion than transfers crossing site boundaries?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') = json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') THEN 'intra-site' ELSE 'inter-site' END AS transfer_scope, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size_bytes, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_throughput_bytes_per_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY transfer_scope ORDER BY transfer_scope

question: For completed FileRead and FileWrite events combined and grouped by site, what is the read operation count, write operation count, total bytes read, total bytes written, and the ratio of total bytes read to total bytes written — revealing which sites are net data consumers versus net data producers and how heavily each site's storage devices are exercised in each direction?
sql: SELECT site, SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'FileRead' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS read_count, SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'FileWrite' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS write_count, SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'FileRead' THEN file_size ELSE 0 END) AS total_bytes_read, SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'FileWrite' THEN file_size ELSE 0 END) AS total_bytes_written, CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'FileRead' THEN file_size ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'FileWrite' THEN file_size ELSE 0 END), 0) AS read_write_byte_ratio FROM (SELECT EVENT AS event_type, json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) AS file_size FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT IN ('FileRead', 'FileWrite') AND STATE = 'Finished') sub GROUP BY site ORDER BY total_bytes_read DESC

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped by site, what is the total job count, count and fraction of jobs that experienced any resource contention (resource_waiting_queue_time greater than zero), average execution duration for uncontended jobs, and average execution duration for contended jobs — revealing which sites suffer the highest resource contention rates and whether contention measurably inflates actual execution time?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS total_jobs, SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.resource_waiting_queue_time') AS REAL) > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS contended_jobs, CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.resource_waiting_queue_time') AS REAL) > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / COUNT(*) AS contention_rate, AVG(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.resource_waiting_queue_time') AS REAL) = 0 THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) END) AS avg_duration_uncontended, AVG(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.resource_waiting_queue_time') AS REAL) > 0 THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) END) AS avg_duration_contended FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY contention_rate DESC

question: For completed FileTransfer events grouped into network latency tiers (below 0.001 seconds, 0.001 to 0.01 seconds, 0.01 to 0.1 seconds, and 0.1 seconds and above), what is the transfer count, average file size in bytes, average transfer duration, average effective throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), and average declared bandwidth — revealing how network latency tiers relate to observed transfer performance and whether high-latency links consistently suppress throughput even when declared bandwidth is high?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.latency') AS REAL) < 0.001 THEN '1_below_0.001s' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.latency') AS REAL) < 0.01 THEN '2_0.001_to_0.01s' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.latency') AS REAL) < 0.1 THEN '3_0.01_to_0.1s' ELSE '4_0.1s_and_above' END AS latency_tier, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size_bytes, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_throughput_bytes_per_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_bandwidth FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY latency_tier ORDER BY latency_tier

question: For completed JobAllocation events grouped by site and host, what is the allocation count, average site_cpu_util, average grid_cpu_util, average site_storage_util, average grid_storage_util, and the average difference between site_cpu_util and grid_cpu_util at allocation completion — revealing which individual hosts are systematically over-loaded relative to grid-wide averages at the moment jobs are assigned to them?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, json_extract(METADATA, '$.host') AS host, COUNT(*) AS allocation_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_cpu_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_storage_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_storage_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL) - CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_cpu_util_diff FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobAllocation' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site, host ORDER BY avg_site_cpu_util DESC

question: For files appearing in both completed FileRead and completed FileTransfer events, grouped by file name, what is the local read count, network transfer count, average file size in bytes, average local read duration, average network transfer duration, and ratio of average transfer duration to average read duration — identifying which shared data objects incur the steepest network-versus-disk delivery penalty and whether heavily read files are also the most heavily transferred?
sql: SELECT r.file AS file_name, r.read_count, t.transfer_count, r.avg_size_bytes, r.avg_read_duration, t.avg_transfer_duration, CASE WHEN r.avg_read_duration > 0 THEN t.avg_transfer_duration / r.avg_read_duration ELSE NULL END AS transfer_to_read_ratio FROM (SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file') AS file, COUNT(*) AS read_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_size_bytes, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_read_duration FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY json_extract(METADATA, '$.file')) r JOIN (SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file') AS file, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_transfer_duration FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY json_extract(METADATA, '$.file')) t ON r.file = t.file ORDER BY transfer_to_read_ratio DESC LIMIT 15

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped by site and by whether total_queue_time is exactly zero (immediate dispatch) or greater than zero (delayed dispatch), what is the job count, average execution duration, average cost, average flops, and average grid_cpu_util — revealing whether jobs dispatched without any queuing delay achieve better execution efficiency and lower cost than those that waited, and which sites most frequently dispatch jobs immediately?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) = 0 THEN 'immediate' ELSE 'queued' END AS dispatch_type, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_execution_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)) AS avg_flops, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site, dispatch_type ORDER BY site, dispatch_type

question: For completed FileWrite events grouped by file name, what are the top 10 most heavily written files by total bytes written, showing write count, total bytes written, average write duration, average disk_write_bw, and count of distinct sites where the file was written — identifying which output data objects dominate storage write workloads across the grid and whether they are concentrated at single sites or distributed widely?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file') AS file_name, COUNT(*) AS write_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_written, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_write_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_disk_write_bw, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) AS distinct_site_count FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY json_extract(METADATA, '$.file') ORDER BY total_bytes_written DESC LIMIT 10

question: For each job that has both a JobAllocation Started event and a completed JobExecution event, grouped by site, what is the job count, average total pipeline time from allocation start to execution finish in simulation clock seconds, average execution duration, and average compute fraction (execution duration divided by total pipeline time) — revealing which sites' jobs spend the highest and lowest proportions of their end-to-end lifecycle in active computation versus allocation and staging overhead?
sql: SELECT json_extract(exec_fin.METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(exec_fin.TIME - alloc_start.min_alloc_start_time) AS avg_pipeline_time, AVG(CAST(json_extract(exec_fin.METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_execution_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(exec_fin.METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) / NULLIF(exec_fin.TIME - alloc_start.min_alloc_start_time, 0)) AS avg_compute_fraction FROM EVENTS exec_fin JOIN (SELECT JOB_ID, MIN(TIME) AS min_alloc_start_time FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobAllocation' AND STATE = 'Started' GROUP BY JOB_ID) alloc_start ON exec_fin.JOB_ID = alloc_start.JOB_ID WHERE exec_fin.EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND exec_fin.STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_pipeline_time DESC

question: For completed FileTransfer events grouped by source site, what is the transfer count, average declared bandwidth, average effective throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), average bandwidth utilization ratio (effective throughput divided by declared bandwidth), and fraction of transfers where effective throughput falls below 50 percent of declared bandwidth — revealing which sites most consistently fail to exploit their outbound link capacity and where network efficiency is poorest?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') AS source_site, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_bandwidth, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_effective_throughput, AVG((CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_bandwidth_utilization_ratio, SUM(CASE WHEN (CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL) < 0.5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 1.0 / COUNT(*) AS fraction_below_50pct_utilization FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' AND CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) > 0 AND CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL) > 0 GROUP BY source_site ORDER BY avg_bandwidth_utilization_ratio ASC

question: For completed FileRead events grouped by site and disk device, what is the read count, total bytes read, average file size in bytes, average read duration, average disk read bandwidth, and maximum disk read bandwidth — identifying which physical storage devices at each site handle the heaviest local read workloads and which individual disks achieve the highest peak throughput?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk') AS disk, COUNT(*) AS read_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_read, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_read_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_disk_read_bw, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS max_disk_read_bw FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site, disk ORDER BY total_bytes_read DESC

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped by site and retry bucket (zero retries, exactly one retry, two or more retries), what is the job count, average execution duration, average total_queue_time, and average cost — revealing whether retried jobs incur systematically longer queue waits or higher costs and which sites generate the most multi-retry workloads?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) = 0 THEN '0_no_retry' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) = 1 THEN '1_one_retry' ELSE '2plus_retries' END AS retry_bucket, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_execution_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_total_queue_time, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site, retry_bucket ORDER BY site, retry_bucket

question: For FileTransfer Started events grouped by destination site, what is the transfer count, average link load at transfer initiation, maximum link load, fraction of transfers initiated with link load above 0.7, average declared bandwidth, and average file size in bytes — revealing which sites most frequently receive inbound transfers onto already-congested network links and where destination-side saturation is the norm rather than the exception?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') AS destination_site, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load_at_start, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS max_link_load_at_start, SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL) > 0.7 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 1.0 / COUNT(*) AS fraction_high_load, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_bandwidth, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Started' GROUP BY destination_site ORDER BY avg_link_load_at_start DESC

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped by host CPU speed tier (below 1000000000 flops per second, 1000000000 to 10000000000, 10000000000 to 100000000000, and 100000000000 and above), what is the job count, average declared flops, average execution duration, average effective compute rate in flops per second (flops divided by duration), and average cost — revealing how host CPU speed tiers relate to actual computational throughput and whether faster declared hardware delivers proportionally lower job cost?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.speed') AS REAL) < 1000000000 THEN '1_below_1e9' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.speed') AS REAL) < 10000000000 THEN '2_1e9_to_10e9' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.speed') AS REAL) < 100000000000 THEN '3_10e9_to_100e9' ELSE '4_above_100e9' END AS speed_tier, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_flops, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_execution_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_effective_compute_rate, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' AND CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) > 0 AND CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.speed') AS REAL) > 0 GROUP BY speed_tier ORDER BY speed_tier

question: For the top 10 most frequently read files in completed FileRead events, what is the file name, read count, count of distinct sites where it was read, count of distinct hosts that read it, total bytes read, average read duration, and average effective read throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration) — identifying the most contended local data objects across the grid and whether widely-distributed files achieve higher or lower average read performance?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file') AS file_name, COUNT(*) AS read_count, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) AS distinct_sites, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.host')) AS distinct_hosts, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_read, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_read_throughput FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY file_name ORDER BY read_count DESC LIMIT 10

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped by number of cores used, what is the job count, average declared flops, average execution duration, average effective compute rate in flops per second (flops divided by duration), average cost, and average cost per declared flop (cost divided by flops) — revealing whether multi-core jobs achieve proportionally better throughput and whether more cores translates to lower or higher cost efficiency?
sql: SELECT CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cores') AS INTEGER) AS cores, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)) AS avg_flops, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_compute_rate, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost_per_flop FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY cores ORDER BY cores

question: For completed FileWrite events grouped by disk device, what is the write count, count of distinct sites where the device appears, total bytes written, average write duration, average effective write throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), and average declared disk_write_bw — identifying which physical storage devices carry the heaviest write workloads and whether high-throughput devices consistently achieve throughput closer to their declared bandwidth?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk') AS disk, COUNT(*) AS write_count, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) AS distinct_sites, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_written, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_write_throughput, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_disk_write_bw FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY disk ORDER BY total_bytes_written DESC

question: For completed FileTransfer events grouped into declared bandwidth tiers (below 100000000 bytes per second, 100000000 to 1000000000 bytes per second, and 1000000000 bytes per second and above), what is the transfer count, average file size in bytes, average transfer duration, average effective throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), and average bandwidth utilization ratio (effective throughput divided by declared bandwidth) — revealing whether higher-capacity links achieve proportionally better utilization or whether other factors suppress efficiency on fast links?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL) < 100000000 THEN 'below_100M' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL) < 1000000000 THEN '100M_to_1G' ELSE '1G_and_above' END AS bandwidth_tier, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_effective_throughput, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_bw_utilization_ratio FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY bandwidth_tier ORDER BY MIN(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL))

question: For completed JobExecution events grouped by site and whether grid_storage_util at completion exceeds 0.75 (high grid storage pressure) or not, what is the job count, average execution duration, average total_queue_time, and average cost — revealing whether grid-wide storage saturation at job completion time correlates with longer execution times or elevated scheduling delays, and which sites are most exposed to this storage-pressure effect?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL) > 0.75 THEN 'high_grid_storage' ELSE 'normal_grid_storage' END AS storage_pressure, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_total_queue_time, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site, storage_pressure ORDER BY site, storage_pressure
